sections.heading: TNM
sections.content: AJCC 8th Edition (2017) TNM Staging System for Pancreatic Cancer. T stages: TX (primary tumor cannot be assessed), T0 (no evidence of primary tumor), Tis (carcinoma in situ, includes high-grade PanIN-3, IPMN with high-grade dysplasia, MCN with high-grade dysplasia), T1 (tumor ≤2 cm in greatest dimension), T1a (≤0.5 cm), T1b (>0.5 cm and <1 cm), T1c (1–2 cm), T2 (>2 cm and ≤4 cm), T3 (>4 cm), T4 (involves celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and/or common hepatic artery, regardless of size). N stages: NX (cannot be assessed), N0 (no regional lymph node metastases), N1 (metastasis in 1–3 regional lymph nodes), N2 (metastasis in ≥4 regional lymph nodes). M stages: M0 (no distant metastasis), M1 (distant metastasis, including non-regional lymph node metastasis). Stage groups: Stage 0 (Tis N0 M0), Stage IA (T1 N0 M0), Stage IB (T2 N0 M0), Stage IIA (T3 N0 M0), Stage IIB (T1-3 N1 M0), Stage III (T1-3 N2 M0 or T4 Any N M0), Stage IV (Any T Any N M1).

t_stages.description: Carcinoma in situ (includes high-grade PanIN-3, IPMN with high-grade dysplasia, MCN with high-grade dysplasia)
Tumor >0.5 cm and <1 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor >2 cm and ≤4 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor involves the celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and/or common hepatic artery, regardless of size

n_stages.description: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed

m_stages.description: Distant metastasis (including non-regional lymph node metastasis)
